_instructions: FINAL ANNOTATION v3. Breda et al. 2023 Science. Fully annotated from main text + supplementary materials + patent US10,221,127 analysis. HIGH confidence on: molar ratio (50:10:38.5:1.5), DSPC helper lipid, particle characterization (~80nm, PDI 0.02-0.06, EE ~95%), antibody clones (2B8/104D2/104), conjugation chemistry (DSPE-PEG-mal + SATA-thiol). MEDIUM confidence on: specific ionizable lipid (ALC-0307 vs ALC-0315), PEG-lipid identity. NULL: zeta potential, N/P ratio.

paper_metadata.title: In vivo hematopoietic stem cell modification by mRNA delivery

ionizable_lipid.name: Acuitas proprietary ionizable lipid (likely ALC-0307 per Tombacz 2021; ALC-0315 also possible)
ionizable_lipid.source: Patent US10,221,127 covers a family of ionizable lipids. Tombacz 2021 names ALC-0307 as default. Cannot confirm which specific variant Breda used. Actively protected by Acuitas.

helper_lipid.name: DSPC (1,2-distearoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine)
helper_lipid.source: Supp methods say phosphatidylcholine. DSPC is the standard PC in all known Acuitas formulations (Onpattro, BNT162b2, Tombacz 2021). No Acuitas publication uses an alternative PC.

peg_lipid.name: PEG2000-lipid (likely ALC-0159 or PEG-DMG based on Acuitas platform)
peg_lipid.source: Not named in paper or supp. Acuitas BNT162b2 uses ALC-0159 (PEG2000-C18). Other Acuitas formulations use PEG-DMG (PEG2000-C14). Certainly a PEG2000 variant.

physicochemical._note: Approximate values from supp methods: ~80nm, PDI 0.02-0.06, EE ~95%. Measured by DLS (Zetasizer Nano ZS) and Ribogreen assay.

manufacturing.details: Ethanolic lipid mixture rapidly mixed with aqueous mRNA at acidic pH (self-assembly). Characterized by DLS (Zetasizer Nano ZS) and Ribogreen assay. Antibody conjugated post-formation via SATA-maleimide chemistry with DSPE-PEG-mal post-insertion.

targeting.conjugation_method: DSPE-PEG-maleimide post-insertion + SATA-thiol on antibody -> thioether conjugation. Purified by Sepharose CL-4B gel filtration.

high_confidence: Molar ratio 50:10:38.5:1.5 (Tombacz + Onpattro benchmark + patent family)
mRNA: m1psi-modified, CleanCap AG cap1, cellulose purified
Manufacturing: microfluidic mixing, ethanolic lipids + aqueous mRNA at acidic pH
medium_confidence: Ionizable lipid identity (ALC-0307 vs ALC-0315 vs other Acuitas variant)
PEG-lipid identity (ALC-0159 vs PEG-DMG vs PEG-DOMG)
null_still_missing: RNA:total lipid weight ratio (Tombacz says ~0.04 wt/wt but not confirmed for Breda)

molar_ratio.source: Tombacz 2021 (same lab/platform) + canonical Acuitas/Onpattro ratio confirmed across patent family US10,221,127 and multiple publications. Supp methods confirm 4-component system.

confidence.notes: Negative control formulation. Same base LNP, non-targeting antibody.

confidence.notes: Naked LNP control. Used only in vitro (Fig 1A-B).

confidence.notes: Used for human SCD base editing experiments (Fig 4). Anti-human CD117 antibody clone not specified in main text.

molar_ratio.source: Tombacz 2021 (same lab/platform) + canonical Acuitas/Onpattro ratio confirmed across patent family US10,221,127 and multiple publications. Supp methods confirm 4-component system.

efficacy.protein_expression: Highest among all formulations at all doses (Fig 1A). 500-fold higher than CD45/LNP when normalized to target cell frequency.
efficacy.other_metric: 500-fold higher normalized luciferase vs CD45/LNP in WBM
efficacy.other_metric_name: Normalized luciferase activity ratio vs CD45/LNP

toxicity.other_aes: No consistent effect on cell viability across formulations (fig S1B-D)

confidence.notes: Luciferase activity directly measured. n=3 replicates. p<0.0001 by Dunnett's after two-way ANOVA.

efficacy.protein_expression: Dramatically higher in Lin- vs WBM. 700-fold higher than CD45/LNP when normalized to target cell frequency.
efficacy.other_metric: 700-fold higher normalized luciferase vs CD45/LNP in Lin-
efficacy.other_metric_name: Normalized luciferase activity ratio vs CD45/LNP

model.cell_line: Lin- BM cells, LSK subset analyzed

efficacy.other_metric: 88.5% at 90h vs 43.5% at 18h for 0.1ug dose in WBM. 2.5-fold more effective than CD45/LNP at 0.1ug in LSK. Saturation at 0.1ug — no significant increase at 0.5 or 1.0ug.
efficacy.other_metric_name: ZsGreen+ in WBM at 90h (persistence after LNP removal)

toxicity.other_aes: No consistent effect on cell viability

confidence.notes: n=3 replicates per condition. Multiple timepoints. Saturation at low dose explicitly stated. p values from Dunnett's after two-way ANOVA.

model.strain: Ai14 BM cells transplanted into C57BL/6 CD45.1 recipients (lethally irradiated)

efficacy.other_metric: 95% LT-HSC editing at both 0.1 and 1ug. >=99% RBC and leukocyte editing at 0.05-1ug. 91.8% at 0.01ug. IgG control: 13.5% (0.1ug) and 20% (1ug). Donor chimerism >94%. Secondary transplant phenocopied primary. Multilineage editing: Gr1+, CD3+, B220+, erythroid all edited.
efficacy.other_metric_name: tdTomato+ in LT-HSC, multilineage, secondary transplant

biodistribution.measurement_method: flow cytometry (tdTomato+) in BM subsets at 4-month endpoint
biodistribution._note: Spleen editing comparable to WBM. PCR confirmed 271bp edited band in BM and spleen DNA.

confidence.notes: n=4-5 mice per cohort. 16-week endpoint. Confirmed by colony assay, PCR, secondary transplant. p<0.0001 by Tukey's after one-way ANOVA.

efficacy.protein_expression: Luciferase activity detected in femur (bone marrow) for CD117/LNP but NOT for IgG/LNP. Comparable liver signal for both.

biodistribution._note: Qualitative: CD117/LNP shows BM signal, IgG does not. Both show comparable liver signal. (Fig 3A)

confidence.notes: Qualitative bioluminescence imaging. Clear BM signal with CD117 but not IgG control.

dosing.timepoint_hours: Endpoint: 16 weeks (4 months). Peripheral blood monitored at 4, 8, 12, 16 weeks.

efficacy.other_metric: 3-fold higher than IgG control (55% vs 19%). All peripheral blood lineages significantly higher: Gr1+ ~60-65%, CD3+ ~55-60%, B220+ ~60%. 5.5-fold increase in LT-HSC editing vs 1ug dose. EPCR+ LT-HSC editing: 46%±14 (comparable to SLAM 55%±10). Secondary transplant: 52% LT-HSC editing (phenocopied primary).
efficacy.other_metric_name: tdTomato+ in LT-HSC, multilineage, secondary transplant, EPCR+ subset

biodistribution._note: Liver: 76-79% editing (comparable between CD117 and IgG — non-specific ApoE uptake). Lung: 7-fold higher CD117 vs IgG; ~8% lung cells are cKit+, ~90% of lung cKit+ were edited. Testes: no significant editing. Germline: 0/50 offspring tdTomato+.

toxicity._note: No toxicity reported for Cre experiments. Toxicity only noted for PUMA experiments.

confidence.notes: n=5 mice per cohort. 16-week endpoint. Confirmed by CFU, PCR, secondary transplant, EPCR+ subset analysis. p<0.001 to p<0.0001.

efficacy.other_metric: 5.5-fold lower than 5ug dose. ~10% SLAM LT-HSC editing estimated from Fig 3G.

confidence.notes: n=7 mice. SLAM LT-HSC value estimated from Fig 3G (~10%). The 5.5-fold relationship to the 5ug group is explicitly stated.

payload.specific_cargo: ABE8e-NRCH Cas9 mRNA + sgRNA targeting HBB E6V (5-UUCUCCACAGGAGUCAGGUG-3). ABE mRNA and sgRNA in separate hCD117/LNPs at 1:1 weight ratio. sgRNA from Synthego.

model.cell_line: Primary SCD erythroid progenitor cells from 4 separate donors

dosing._note: 3-10 pg/cell dose at 1:1 wt ratio ABE:sgRNA. 6h exposure. 1.5E6 cells/mL.

efficacy.editing_method: base editing allele frequency (Sanger sequencing with EditR)
efficacy.protein_expression: Up to 91.7% HBB-G Makassar (non-pathogenic) of beta-like globin by RP-HPLC. 34.5% at 3pg/cell, 86.7% at 10pg/cell.
efficacy.other_metric: Near-complete absence of sickled cells under hypoxia. Sickling reduced from ~25-30% (unedited) to ~5% (edited). R-squared = 0.9792 for editing-to-protein correlation. Slope ~1.04 (near 1:1).
efficacy.other_metric_name: Sickling assay, HBB-G protein level by RP-HPLC, editing-protein correlation

toxicity.other_aes: No alteration of viability or proliferation at 3-10 pg/cell (fig S8C-D)

confidence.notes: 4 separate SCD donor samples. 88% editing at 10pg/cell. Functional validation by sickling assay under hypoxia. p<0.0001. R²=0.9792 for DNA-protein correlation.

payload.specific_cargo: PUMA (p53 up-regulated modulator of apoptosis) mRNA — pro-apoptotic BH3-only protein

model.strain: C57BL/6 CD45.2 BM treated, transplanted into C57BL/6 CD45.1 recipients (lethally irradiated), competitive with GFP+ untreated BM

efficacy.editing_method: competitive transplant — flow cytometry for GFP- (PUMA-treated) vs GFP+ (untreated) donor cells
efficacy.other_metric: 100% PUMA-treated mice died within 2 weeks (complete HSC depletion). 50/75% mix: <0.5% GFP- donor cells at 4 months (vs expected 50-75%). Essentially complete depletion. Donor chimerism >=94% across all groups.

toxicity.other_aes: Mice receiving only PUMA-treated BM died within 2 weeks (expected — BM failure from HSC depletion)
toxicity._note: Severity is 'severe' in terms of HSC depletion (which is the intended effect), not off-target toxicity

confidence.notes: n=4-8 per cohort. Competitive transplant design with clear functional readout. p<0.0001.

payload.specific_cargo: PUMA mRNA with miR-122 binding sites in 3'UTR (PUMA-miR) for liver detargeting

model.strain: C57BL/6 recipients, GFP+ C57BL/6 BM donors

dosing.timepoint_hours: PUMA given 7 days before BM infusion. Endpoint: 16 weeks.
dosing._note: 0.05 mg/kg was maximum tolerated dose. 0.15 mg/kg or more was lethal.

efficacy.editing_method: flow cytometry for GFP+ donor engraftment
efficacy.other_metric: 71% decrease in LSK frequency at 6 days. 58% decrease in LT-HSC frequency at 6 days. 3.8% donor BM LSK cells at 16 weeks. Progressive increase and stabilization of GFP+ Gr1+ and RBCs. Unconditioned controls: 0% engraftment. Secondary transplant phenocopied donors.
efficacy.other_metric_name: HSC depletion (% decrease in LSK/LT-HSC) and donor engraftment (% GFP+ in BM)

toxicity.other_aes: At >=0.15 mg/kg: decreased activity, venous congestion of lungs and liver, mortality. miR-122 binding sites incorporated to reduce liver expression.
toxicity._note: 0.05 mg/kg = maximum tolerated dose. Narrow therapeutic window. Toxicity at 3x the effective dose.

confidence.notes: n=3 recipients for conditioning + transplant. n=2 unconditioned controls. Small sample sizes but clear functional results. MTD clearly defined.

blood_disorder_data.genetic_target: HBB (E6V sickle mutation converted to E6A G-Makassar variant)
blood_disorder_data.hsc_subtype_targeted: erythroid progenitor cells (ex vivo on human primary SCD cells)
blood_disorder_data.engraftment_data: 3.8% donor LSK engraftment with in vivo PUMA conditioning (sufficient for SCID cure per refs 22-24)

hemoglobin_data._note: Paper reports HBB-G Makassar (not HbF). Up to 91.7% of beta-like globin converted to non-pathogenic HBB-G. Sickling reduced to ~5% from ~25-30%.

blood_disorder_data._note: Two applications demonstrated: (1) direct base editing of SCD cells ex vivo with hCD117/LNP, (2) non-genotoxic conditioning via PUMA-mediated HSC depletion in vivo

key_findings_for_ml.what_makes_this_formulation_work: CD117 antibody conjugation enables active targeting to HSCs via c-Kit receptor internalization pathway. CD117 naturally internalizes upon ligand (SCF) binding, providing a free endocytic entry mechanism for the entire LNP. This achieves 500-700x higher normalized delivery than CD45-targeted LNPs in vitro, and 3-fold higher LT-HSC editing than IgG controls in vivo. Saturation of target cells at very low doses in vitro (0.1ug) suggests high binding affinity and efficient internalization.
key_findings_for_ml.comparison_to_other_formulations: CD117/LNP >> CD45/LNP >> IgG/LNP >= unmodified LNP. In vitro: 500-700x normalized luciferase advantage over CD45. 2.5x more LSK editing at 0.1ug. In vivo: 55% vs 19% LT-HSC editing (CD117 vs IgG at 5ug). Ex vivo: 95% vs 13.5-20% LT-HSC editing. Key differentiator is the targeting antibody, not the base LNP formulation (which is identical across all variants).
key_findings_for_ml.limitations_noted_by_authors: Major: liver uptake is high and non-specific (76-79% liver editing, comparable between CD117 and IgG). Narrow therapeutic window for PUMA: MTD is 0.05 mg/kg, toxicity at 0.15 mg/kg. miR-122 detargeting only partially addresses liver issue. In vivo editing (55%) substantially lower than ex vivo (95%). Engraftment after PUMA conditioning modest (3.8% donor LSK). No NHP data. Formulation is proprietary (Acuitas), limiting reproducibility.
key_findings_for_ml.data_quality_notes: Generally high quality: multiple confirmation methods (flow cytometry, colony assays, PCR, secondary transplants), adequate group sizes (n=3-8), appropriate controls (IgG isotype, unconjugated, untreated). Weak points: particle characterization in supplementary (not accessible), formulation proprietary/not fully disclosed, some dose groups have small n (n=2-3 for PUMA conditioning). Published in Science with erratum (Fig 2E image duplication, does not affect conclusions).

label_for_ml.primary_metric_used: tdTomato+ in LT-HSC (LSK CD150+CD48-) by flow cytometry
label_for_ml._note: 55% is the in vivo value (most clinically relevant). Ex vivo value is 95%. Both exceed the 30% threshold for 'high'.
